F500 ## $a Accompanying compact discs contain both music and spoken word selection illustrating the text. F500 ## $a Title on accompanying CD: Voices of world literature. F504 ## $a Includes bibliographical references and indexes. F505 0# $a Vol. A. The ancient world -- v. B. The medieval era -- v. C. The early modern period -- v. D. The seventeenth and eighteenth centuries -- v. E. The nineteenth century -- v. F. The twentieth century. F505 2# $a v. A. The ancient world -- Crosscurrents: creation myths and social concerns -- A Babylonian theogony -- The Memphite theology -- The Pyramid texts of Unas -- Hymns from the Rig Veda -- The great hymn to the Aten -- from Enuma Elish: the Babylonian creation epic -- Hesiod -- Genesis -- The ancient Near East -- Poetry of love an devotion -- The song of songs -- The epic of Gilgamesh -- Perspectives: death and immortality -- The book of job -- Perspectives: strangers in a strange land -- The story of Sinuhe -- The two brothers -- The Joseph story -- The book of Ruth -- Classical Greece -- Homer -- Arkhilokhos -- Sppho -- Alkaios -- Pindar -- Aeschylus -- Sophocles -- Perspectives: tyranny and democracy -- Solon -- Herodotus -- Plato -- Euripides -- Aristophanes -- Early South Asia -- The Mahabharata of Vyasa -- The Ramayana of Valmiki -- Perspectives: what is "Literature"? -- Love in a courtly language -- The Tamil anthologies -- The seven hundred songs of Hala -- The hundred poems of Amaru -- Vatsyayana -- Kalidasa -- Perspectives: asceticism, wisdom, and the middle way -- China: the classical tradition -- The book of songs -- Confucius -- Perspectives: Daoism and its ways -- Sima Qian -- Rome and the Roman empire -- Virgil -- Ovid -- Perspectives: the culture of Rome and the beginnings of Christianity -- Augustine -- Maps. F505 2# $a v. B. The medieval era -- Crosscurrents: contact, conflict and conversion -- Medieval China -- Liu Xiang -- Ban Zhao -- Yuan Cai -- Voices of women -- Yuan Zhen -- Tao Qian -- Han-Shan -- Wang Wei -- Li Bo -- Du Fu -- Bo Juyi -- Perspectives: what is literature? -- Li Yu -- Li Qingzhao -- Japan -- Kojiki (record of ancient matters) -- Man'yōshū (collection of myriad leaves) -- Murasaki Shikibu -- Perspectives: courtly women -- Ono no Kamachi -- Michitsuna's mother -- Sei Shōnagon -- Kamo no Chōmei -- Tales of the Heike -- Zeami -- Classical Arabic and Islamic literatures -- Imru' Al-Qays -- Al-Khansa' -- The brigand poets: Al-sa'alik -- The Qur'an -- Hafiz -- Perspectives: poetry, wine, and love -- Abu-Nuwas -- Abu-Tammam -- Al-Buhturi -- Ibn Al-rumi -- Al-Mutanabbi -- Ibn Zaydun -- Al-Jahiz -- The thousand and one nights -- Jalal Al-din Rumi -- Perspectives: asceticism, sufism, and wisdom -- Al-Hallaj -- Al-Niffari -- Ibn 'Arabi -- Firdawsi -- The epic of Son-Jara -- Medieval Europe -- Beowulf -- The poem of the Cid -- Perspectives: Iberia, the meeting of three worlds -- Castilian ballads and traditional songs -- Mozarabic Kharjas -- Ibn Hạzm -- Ibn Rushd (Averroës) -- Solomon Ibn Gabirol -- Yehuda Ha-Levi -- Ramón Llull -- Martin Codax -- Troubadours and Trobairitz -- Walther Von Der Vogelweide -- Marie De France -- Sir Gawain and the Green Knight -- Perspectives: the art of love -- Peter Abelard and Heloïse -- from The play of Adam -- Dante Alighieri -- Geoffrey Chaucer -- François Villon. F505 2# $a v. C. The early modern period -- Crosscurrents: the vernacular revolution -- Vernacular writing in South Asia -- Basavanna -- Mahadeviyakka -- Kabir -- Tukaram -- Kshetrayya -- Wu Cheng'en -- The rise of the vernacular in Europe -- Biblical translations -- attacking and defending the vernacular bible -- women and the vernacular -- Early modern Europe -- Giovanni Boccaccio -- Marguerite de Navarre -- Francis Petrarch -- After the death of my lady Laura -- Perspectives: lyric sequences and self-definition -- Louise Labé -- Michelangelo Buonarroti -- Vittoria Colonna -- William Shakespeare -- Jan Kochanowski -- Sor Juana Inés de la Cruz -- Niccolò Machiavelli -- Sir Thomas More -- Perspectives: Literature of religious crisis -- Desiderius Erasmus -- Martin Luther -- Thomas Müntzer -- Saint Teresa of Avila -- Saint John of the Cross -- Domenico Scandella -- François Rabelais -- Luís Vaz de Camões -- Michel de Montaigne -- Miguel de Cervantes Saavedra -- Lope Féliz de Vega Carpio -- William Shakespeare -- John Donne -- Anne Bradstreet -- John Milton -- Mesoamerica: before Columbus and after Cortés -- The legend of the suns -- from Popol Vuh: the Mayan council book -- Songs of the Aztec nobility -- Perspectives: the conquest and its aftermath -- Christopher Columbus -- Bernal Díaz del Castillo -- Bernardino de Sahagún -- from The Aztec-Spanish dialogues of 1524 -- Hernando Ruiz de Alarcón -- Bartolomé de las casas -- Sor Juana Inéz de la Cruz. F505 2# $a v. D. The seventeenth and eighteenth centuries -- Crosscurrents: courts and commoners -- The world the Mughals made -- Zahiruddin Muhammad Babur -- Jahangir -- Mirza Muhammad Rafi "Sauda" -- Mir Muhammad Taqi "Mir" -- Banarasidas -- Chikamatsu Mon'zaemon -- Ihara Saikaku -- Cao Xueqin -- The Ottoman Empire -- Mihri Khatun -- Fuzuli -- Nedîm -- Lady Mary Wortley Montagu -- The age of the Enlightenment -- Jean-Baptiste Poquelin [Molière] -- The school for wives -- Perspectives: court culture and female authorship -- Madeleine de Scudéry -- Marie-Madeleine Pioche de la Vergne, Comtesse de Lafayette -- Marie de Rabutin-Chantal, Marquise de Sévigné -- Elizabeth Charlotte von der Pfalz -- Katherine Philips -- Mary, Lady Chudleigh -- Anne Finch, countess of Winchilsea -- Jonathan Swift -- Lady Mary Worley Montagu -- Ann Yearsley -- Aphra Behn -- Jonathan Swift -- Gulliver's travels -- Perspectives: Journeys in search of the self -- Evliya Çelebi -- Matsuo Bashō -- Charles de Secondat, Baron de la Bréde et de Montesquieu -- Françoise de Graffigny -- Denis Diderot -- Olaudah Equiano -- Francois-Marie Arouet [Voltaire] -- Alexander Pope -- Lorenzo de Ponte -- Perspectives: liberty and libertines -- Ihara Saikaku -- Tsangyang Gyatso -- John Wilmot, Earl of Rochester -- Eliza Haywood -- Jean-Jacques Rousseau -- Donatien-Alphonse-François, Marquis de Sade -- Mary Wollstonecraft -- Anna Letitia Barbauld -- Immanuel Kant. F505 2# $a v. E. The nineteenth century -- Crosscurrents: the folk and their tales -- Aesop's fables -- Panchatantra -- Jean de la Fontaine -- The Pali Jatakas -- Joel Chandler Harris -- Charles Perrault -- Benedikte Naubert -- Jacob and Wilheilm Grimm -- Coyote Tales -- Mark Twain -- William Wordsworth -- Perspectives: Romantic nature -- Jean-Jacques Rousseau -- Immanuel Kant -- John Keats -- Annette von Droste-Hülshoff -- Giacomo Leopardi -- Ralph Waldo Emerson -- Henry David Thoreau -- Johann Wolfgang Goethe -- To the moon -- Erlking -- Mignon -- Dusk descended from on high -- Blissful yearning -- George Gordon, Lord Byron -- Ghalib -- Alexander Sergeyevich Pushkin -- Perspectives: the national poet -- Nguyen Du -- Anna Letitia Barbauld -- Adam Mickiewicz -- Dionysios Solomos -- Ralph Waldo Emerson -- Walt Whitman -- Perspectives: on the colonial frontier -- Kikhail Lermontov -- Domingo Sarmiento -- Charles A. Eastman (Ohiyesa) -- Hawaiian songs -- José Rizal -- The romantic fantastic -- Samuel Taylor Coleridge -- Ludwig Tieck -- Honoré de Balzac -- Edgar Allan Poe -- Gustave Flaubert -- Perspectives: Occidentalism : Europe through foreign eyes -- Najaf Kuli Mirza -- Mustafa Sami Effendi -- Hattori Bushō -- Okakura Kakuzo -- Elizabeth Barrett Browning -- Charles Baudelaire -- Leo Tolstoy -- Fyodor Dostoevsky -- Hathali Nez and Washington Matthews -- Herman Melville -- Frederick Douglass -- Harriet Jacobs -- Emily Dickinson -- Joaquim María Machado de Assis -- Charlotte Perkins Gilman -- Rubén Darío -- Henrik Ibsen -- Higuchi Ichiyo -- Liu E -- Anton Chekhov -- Rabindranath Tagore. F505 2# $a v. F. The Twentieth century -- Crosscurrents: the art of the manifesto -- Filippo Tommaso Marinetti -- Tristan Tzara -- André Breton -- Mina Loy -- Yokomitsu Riichi -- Oswald de Andrade -- André Breton -- Hu Shi -- Joseph Conrad -- Premchand -- Lu Xun -- James Joyce -- Virginia Woolf -- Akutagawa Ryunosuke -- Perspectives: modernist memory -- T.S. Eliot -- Constantine Cavafy -- Claude McKay -- Federico García Lorca -- Carlos Drummond de Andrade -- Emile Habiby -- Octovio Paz -- Franz Kafka -- Anna Akhmatova -- Perspectives: modernism and revolution in Russia -- Vladimir Mayakovski -- Boris Pasternak Andrei Bely -- Marina Tsvetaeva -- Osip Mandelstam -- William Butler Yeats -- Rainer Maria Rilke -- Perspectives: poetry about poetry -- Ezra Pound -- Eugenio Montale -- Fernando Pessoa -- Pablo Neruda -- Wallace Stevens -- Nazim Hikmet -- Bei Dao -- Daniel David Moses -- Bertolt Brecht -- Primo Levi -- Perspectives: echoes of war -- Yosano Akiko -- Rupert Brooke -- Wilfred Owen -- Yukio Mishima -- Paul Celan -- Zbigniew Herbert -- Alejo Carpentier -- Nazim Hikmet -- Ingeborg Bachmann -- Yehuda Amichai -- Samuel Beckett -- Perspectives: cosmopolitan exiles -- César Vallejo -- Vladimir Nabokov -- Czeslaw Milosz -- V.S. Naipaul -- Adonis (Ali Ahmad Sa'id) -- Jorge Luis Borges -- Naguib Mahfouz -- Perspectives: the 1001 nights in the twentieth century -- Güneli Gün -- John Barth -- Italo Calvino -- Léopold Sédar Senghor -- Aimé Césaire -- James Baldwin -- Gerald Vizenor -- Perspectives: indigenous cultures in the twentieth century. F505 2# $a Oodgeroo of the tribe Noonuccal -- Archie Weller -- Paula Gunn Allen -- Leslie Marmon Silko -- N. rdf:Description.rdamd:P30182: volumes : illustrations (some color), maps ; 24 cm + audio discs (digital ; 4 /34 in.)
rdf:Description.rdamd:P30137: Vol B has publisher: Pearson Longman.
Accompanying compact discs contain both music and spoken word selection illustrating the text.
Title on accompanying CD: Voices of world literature.

Contents: Vol. A. The ancient world -- v. B. The medieval era -- v. C. The early modern period -- v. D. The seventeenth and eighteenth centuries -- v. E. The nineteenth century -- v. F. The twentieth century.

Partial contents: v. A. The ancient world -- Crosscurrents: creation myths and social concerns -- A Babylonian theogony -- The Memphite theology -- The Pyramid texts of Unas -- Hymns from the Rig Veda -- The great hymn to the Aten -- from Enuma Elish: the Babylonian creation epic -- Hesiod -- Genesis -- The ancient Near East -- Poetry of love an devotion -- The song of songs -- The epic of Gilgamesh -- Perspectives: death and immortality -- The book of job -- Perspectives: strangers in a strange land -- The story of Sinuhe -- The two brothers -- The Joseph story -- The book of Ruth -- Classical Greece -- Homer -- Arkhilokhos -- Sppho -- Alkaios -- Pindar -- Aeschylus -- Sophocles -- Perspectives: tyranny and democracy -- Solon -- Herodotus -- Plato -- Euripides -- Aristophanes -- Early South Asia -- The Mahabharata of Vyasa -- The Ramayana of Valmiki -- Perspectives: what is "Literature"? -- Love in a courtly language -- The Tamil anthologies -- The seven hundred songs of Hala -- The hundred poems of Amaru -- Vatsyayana -- Kalidasa -- Perspectives: asceticism, wisdom, and the middle way -- China: the classical tradition -- The book of songs -- Confucius -- Perspectives: Daoism and its ways -- Sima Qian -- Rome and the Roman empire -- Virgil -- Ovid -- Perspectives: the culture of Rome and the beginnings of Christianity -- Augustine -- Maps.

Partial contents: v. B.
